docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text: edit

docs.title: GitHub Actions MkDocs Workflow
docs.text: This document explains the GitHub Actions workflow that builds and deploys the SL documentation site using MkDocs Material theme.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
When you push changes to the main branch, GitHub Actions automatically: 1. Checks out the repository 2. Installs Python and MkDocs with the Material theme 3. Builds the markdown files into a static HTML site 4. Deploys the built site to the gh-pages branch

GitHub Pages then serves the gh-pages branch at https://sl.2cld.net.

docs.title: Workflow File Explained
docs.text:
The workflow lives at .github/workflows/mkdocs.yml.

name: Deploy MkDocs to GitHub Pages

Display name shown in the GitHub Actions tab.

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

Trigger: runs every time code is pushed to the main branch. Pull requests or other branches won't trigger a deploy.

permissions:
  contents: write

Grants the workflow permission to write to the repository. Required because mkdocs gh-deploy pushes the built site to the gh-pages branch.

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

Runs on a fresh Ubuntu virtual machine provided by GitHub (free for public repos).

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

Checks out your repository code so the workflow can access the markdown files and mkdocs config.

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'

Installs the latest Python 3.x on the runner. MkDocs requires Python.

      - run: pip install mkdocs-material

Installs MkDocs and the Material theme. The mkdocs-material package includes mkdocs as a dependency.

      - run: mkdocs gh-deploy --force --config-file .mkdocs/mkdocs.yml

Builds the site and pushes it to the gh-pages branch. - --force overwrites the gh-pages branch completely each time - --config-file .mkdocs/mkdocs.yml points to our config (which is in a subfolder because mkdocs requires docs_dir to be a child of the config location)

docs.title: MkDocs Configuration
docs.text: The config lives at .mkdocs/mkdocs.yml and controls: - docs_dir: .. - tells mkdocs to look for markdown files in the repo root - site_dir: ../site - where the built HTML goes (locally) - nav: - defines the sidebar navigation structure - theme: - Material theme with dark mode and search

docs.title: GitHub Pages Settings
docs.text:
After the first workflow run, configure GitHub Pages: 1. Go to repo Settings > Pages 2. Set Source to Deploy from a branch 3. Set Branch to gh-pages / (root) 4. Save

The CNAME file is automatically copied to the gh-pages branch by mkdocs.

docs.title: How to Revert to Plain GitHub Pages
docs.text: If you want to go back to GitHub serving raw markdown (no MkDocs):

docs.title: Step 1: Change GitHub Pages source branch
docs.text:
1. Go to repo Settings > Pages
  2. Change Branch from gh-pages to main
  3. Set folder to / (root)
  4. Click Save

docs.title: Step 2: Disable the workflow
docs.text: Either delete .github/workflows/mkdocs.yml or disable it: 1. Go to repo Actions tab 2. Click on "Deploy MkDocs to GitHub Pages" workflow 3. Click the ... menu > Disable workflow

docs.title: Step 3: (Optional) Delete the gh-pages branch
docs.text:
git push origin --delete gh-pages

After these steps, GitHub Pages will serve your raw markdown files directly from the main branch, just like before.

docs.title: Local Preview
docs.text:
To preview the site locally before pushing:

pip install mkdocs-material
python -m mkdocs serve -f .mkdocs/mkdocs.yml

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser.

⚠ CONFIRM: LAN IP observed as both 192.168.0.143 and 192.168.0.141 on different dates. This machine uses DHCP (no static reservation). The IP will drift unless a MAC reservation is added on the router for MAC B0-83-FE-65-80-80. Recommend adding a DHCP reservation on 192.168.0.1 to lock this down.
